zh.Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國諾廷加姆爾素維爾
zh.Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Engl: 英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼
英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼
zh.lifeSummary: 當William BALLARD於大約1518年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼時，他的父親John Ballard38歲，母親Margaret Hussey27歲。他於1540年與Agnes ROBERTSON在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1605年在英國諾廷加姆爾素維爾享年88歲。

zh-hans.Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Engl: 英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼
英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William BALLARD于大约1518年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼时，他的父亲John Ballard38岁，母亲Margaret Hussey27岁。他于1540年与Agnes ROBERTSON在英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1605年在英国諾廷加姆爾素維爾享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: BALLARD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ballard ‘bald-headed man’ (compare Bald 3).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Balhard, composed of the elements bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’, and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Ballor .

variants.name: Ballon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Sergio, Alfonso, Cesar, Conchita, Emigdio, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Jose Luis, Julio, Ruben.  Jewish Hyman, Isadore.

French and English (Middlesex): from the Old French personal name Ballon, Ballun, derived from the oblique stem of ancient Germanic Ballo, a short form of compound names based on the element bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’ (see Ballard ).

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Ballor
meanings: Altered form of French Ballard 2, reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. It is found mainly in MI.

variants.name: Bollard
meanings: English (Northamptonshire) and Irish: probably a variant of   Ballard  . According to MacLysaght, this is a surname of Dutch origin which was taken to Ireland early in the 18th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bolohard, composed of the elements bolo ‘friend, brother’ and hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When William BALLARD was born about 1518, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, his father, John Ballard, was 38 and his mother, Margaret Hussey, was 27. He married Agnes ROBERTSON in 1540, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1605, in Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William BALLARD est né environ 1518, à Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John Ballard, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Margaret Hussey, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes ROBERTSON en 1540, à Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1605, à Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 88 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William BALLARD um 1518 in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Ballard 38 und seine Mutter Margaret Hussey 27. Er hat 1540 in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England Agnes ROBERTSON geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1605 im Alter von 88 Jahren in Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William BALLARD è nato il circa 1518 nel Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, John Ballard, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Hussey, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes ROBERTSON nel 1540, nel Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1605, nel Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 88 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William BALLARDが約1518年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイで生まれた時，父John Ballardは38歳，母Margaret Husseyは27歳であった。1540年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイでAgnes ROBERTSONと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1605年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアソウスウエルで88歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William BALLARD(이)가 대략 1518년 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Ballard(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Hussey(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1540년 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 Agnes ROBERTSON(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1605년 영국 노틴그함실르 소우스웰에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William BALLARD nasceu aproximadamente 1518, em Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Ballard, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Hussey, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes ROBERTSON em 1540, em Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1605, em Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 88 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William BALLARD родился около 1518 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Ballard было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Hussey, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes ROBERTSON в 1540 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1605 г. в местности соутвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 88 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William BALLARD nació aproximadamente 1518, en Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Ballard, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Margaret Hussey, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes ROBERTSON en 1540, en Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1605, en Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 88 años.
